seoTitle: How to Verify Bitcoin on Your PayPal Account (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to activate PayPal's crypto hub, complete KYC verification, and unlock your Bitcoin wallet address to buy, receive, or send crypto.
intro: Buying or receiving Bitcoin through PayPal requires clearing a separate identity check beyond your standard account verification. This guide covers activating the Crypto Hub, submitting the required documents, and generating your Bitcoin wallet address. It's meant for anyone getting started with crypto inside their existing PayPal account.

steps.heading: Open the Crypto Hub
steps.body: From the main menu, tap the Finances or Crypto tab and select Bitcoin to trigger the account setup prompt.

steps.heading: Review terms and start verification
steps.body: Accept the crypto terms of service and click Get Started to open the identity form.

steps.heading: Enter your baseline details
steps.body: Provide your date of birth, billing address, and taxpayer identification number.

steps.heading: Upload ID and a selfie
steps.body: Submit a clear scan of your government ID or passport, then complete a real-time biometric selfie to confirm your identity.

steps.heading: Submit and access your wallet
steps.body: Send your verification package for review, and once approved, tap Receive to reveal your unique Bitcoin wallet address.

faq.question: Is crypto verification separate from standard account verification?
faq.answer: Yes, PayPal applies distinct compliance layers specifically for crypto trading beyond your regular account checks.

faq.question: How long does crypto verification take?
faq.answer: Review times vary, but most submissions are processed within a short window after your documents pass initial checks.

faq.question: Can I withdraw Bitcoin to an external wallet?
faq.answer: Yes, once fully verified, PayPal allows external crypto wallet withdrawals for eligible accounts.

faq.question: Do I need a selfie every time I trade?
faq.answer: No, the biometric selfie is typically a one-time step required during initial crypto hub verification.
